CROSS DEFENDANT R & G EXECUTIVE LOUNGE, INC.'s Motion For a)An Order Sealing The November 13 2017 Declaration Of Monica Ip And b) A Protecive Order Limiting The Parties Us Of The November 13 2017 Declaration Of Monica Ip To This Action.

Defendant R & G Executive Lounge, Inc.'s motion for entry of an order sealing the November 13, 2017 declaration of Monica Ip is denied. Substantially all of the compensation and related financial information contained in that declaration was disclosed in the April 20, 2018 declaration of Ms. Ip, which was publicly filed on that date in unredacted form by Defendants and Cross-Complainants Henry Hung and Joe K. Ling in support of their ex parte application for a temporary restraining order and order to show cause re preliminary injunction.

R & G's motion for entry of a protective order limiting the use of the Ip declaration to this action is denied. The declaration does not constitute a Company document produced in discovery in this action, and in any event it has already been provided to Mr. Wong's experts in another matter.

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT R & G EXECUTIVE LOUNGE, INC. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT (Emily Wong)

CROSS DEFENDANT R & G EXECUTIVE LOUNGE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT.

Cross-Defendant R&G Executive Lounge, Inc.'s demurrer to Cross-Complainant's cross-complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the sole cause of action for unreimbursed business expenses under Labor Code section 2802. Labor Code section 2802 does not require an employer to indemnify its employees for expenditures or losses arising out of a lawsuit brought by the employer. (Thornton v. Cal. Unemployment Ins. Appeals Bd. (2012) 204 Cal.App.4th 1403, 1420 ["[U]nder Labor Code section 2802 . . . an employee sued by his employer is not entitled to reimbursement for the costs of successfully defending against the lawsuit because the reimbursement obligation is phrased in terms of indemnity . . . which implies an obligation to pay a judgment entered or expenses incurred in a lawsuit by a third party."]; Nicholas Laboratories LLC v. Chen (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1240, 1251 ["We are not persuaded that the Legislature, in drafting section 2802, intended to depart from the usual meaning of the word 'indemnify' to address 'first party' disputes between employers and employees. . . . The fact that the Legislature did not do so suggests disputes between employers and employees are subject to the ordinary rules applying to the recovery of attorney fees in California litigation."].) Although Joe Ling and Henry Hung's cross-complaint originally pled individual claims in addition to claims pled derivatively on behalf of R&G, Defendants have since voluntarily dismissed the individual claims. (RJN Ex. F.) The sixth and seventh causes of action for fraud and conspiracy to commit breach of fiduciary duty remain alleged on a derivative basis and no other substantive changes have been made to the allegations of Ling and Hung's cross-complaint. (RJN Exs. A, F.) Thus, any expenditures by Cross-Complainant in defense of these claims relate equally to the derivative claims. As a result, Cross-Complainant's claim for unreimbursed business expenses is improperly sought for defending against claims brought by the employer, R&G, rather than claims brought by a third party. The cases relied upon by Cross-Complainant are therefore inapplicable because those cases involve seeking indemnity for defending against third-party lawsuits.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

The fact that the Legislature did not do so suggests disputes between employers and employees are subject to the ordinary rules applying to the recovery of attorney fees in California litigation."].) Although Joe Ling and Henry Hung's cross-complaint originally pled individual claims in addition to claims pled derivatively on behalf of R&G, Defendants have since voluntarily dismissed the individual claims. (RJN Ex. F.) The sixth and seventh causes of action for fraud and conspiracy to commit breach of fiduciary duty remain alleged on a derivative basis and no other substantive changes have been made to the allegations of Ling and Hung's cross-complaint. (RJN Exs. A, F.) Thus, any expenditures by Cross-Complainant in defense of these claims relate equally to the derivative claims. As a result, Cross-Complainant's claim for unreimbursed business expenses is improperly sought for defending against claims brought by the employer, R&G, rather than claims brought by a third party. The cases relied upon by Cross-Complainant are therefore inapplicable because those cases involve seeking indemnity for defending against third-party lawsuits.

Order is signed.

Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jan-30-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jeffrey Kirschenbaum, Esq., Telephone 510-740-9260, appearing for Cross-Complainants/Cross Defendants, Karen Liu, et al;,
Sanjeet S. Ganjam, Esq., Shartsis Friese LLP, Telephone 415-421-6500, appearing for Cross-Complainants Henry Hung, et al;

Ruling- A R G U E D: The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

Cross-defendants Frank Wong, Emily Wong, and Karen Liu's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is denied as to the breach of loyalty cause of action. The cross-complaint alleges conduct "inimical" to R&G's interests within the meaning of Stokes v. Dole Nut Co. (1995) 41 Cal.App.4th 285, 295. (See also Huong Que, Inc. v. Luu (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 400; Rest. 3rd of Agency, 8.05.)

The motion is granted with 20 days leave to amend as to the breach of contract cause of action to allege with more particularity whether the alleged contract was oral or written and the terms of the contract that were allegedly breached.

The motion is denied as to the breach of implied covenant cause of action. The contract sufficiently alleges a contractual relationship based on the employer/employee relationship, and cross-defendants' "unauthorized" acceptance of excessive compensation sufficiently alleges a breach of an implied covenant that thwarts R&G's benefit of the bargain. (Cross-Complaint, 14.)

The motion is denied as to the conversion cause of action. Paragraph 38 of the cross-complaint alleges an identifiable sum to support the claim. (See SP Investment Fund I LLC v. Cattell (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 898, 907.)

The motion is denied as to the money had and received cause of action. (See Stratton v. Hanning (1956) 139 Cal.App.2d 723, 727.)

The motion is denied as to the concealment cause of action. The cross-complaint sufficiently alleges active concealment, which does not require a fiduciary duty, and less particularity in the allegations is acceptable based upon the very nature of a concealment claim. (See Heliotis v. Schuman (1986) 181 Cal.App.3d 646, 651; Tenet Healthsystem Desert, Inc. v. Blue Cross of Cal. (2016) 245 Cal.App.4th 821, 838.)

The motion is denied as to the conspiracy to breach fiduciary duty cause of action. "When the nonfiduciary is an agent or employee of the fiduciary, the nonfiduciary is entitled to the benefit of the "agent's immunity rule" (and thus not liable on a conspiracy theory) unless the nonfiduciary was acting for its own benefit." (Everest Investors 8 v. Whitehall RealEstate Ltd. P'ship XI (2002) 100 Cal.App.4th 1102, 1104.)

The court rejects cross-defendants' statute of limitations arguments because the cross-complaint alleges facts showing that cross-complainants only recently learned of the malfeasance after the preliminary injunction hearing. (Cross-Complaint, 14; Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808.)

The court rejects cross-defendants' voluntary payment and Labor Code 221 defenses. Cross-defendants raise factual issues and "unauthorized" payments do not support the defenses.
